items.title: 'Wholly and solely systemic racism': Anger over closure of Indigenous health hub

children.content: An absence of funding from the ACT and federal governments is forcing the Yerrabi Yurwang Health Hub to close this week, affecting about 400 First Nations patients.

gallery.altText: A woman with glasses in front of an Indigenous artwork.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with glasses in front of an Indigenous artwork.

items.title: ACT police, prosecutors accused of 'egregious conduct' over botched prosecution

children.content: Lawyers for a Canberra man cleared of 14 family violence charges are calling for an apology and compensation, after police found evidence the complainant fabricated some claims.

image.altText: ACT Magistrate Court

items.title: Teenagers brandish knives, hammer at Canberra shopping centre

children.content: Police say they were called to the Canberra Centre about 6:20pm on Friday after reports that a group of young people were involved in a disturbance, brandishing knives and a hammer.

image.altText: A police car with its lights on outside a shopping centre.

items.title: Australia aids Artemis II as NASA astronauts return to the Moon

children.content: Canberra-based communications systems will support NASA's first crewed lunar mission in more than half a century, helping astronauts orbit the Moon and return data.

gallery.altText: Three people look at space communications technology.
Astronauts wearing orange suits inside a spacecraft.

thumbnail.altText: Three people look at space communications technology.
Astronauts wearing orange suits inside a spacecraft.

items.title: End of an era for general store owned by family for 100 years

children.content: The Tharwa General Store — which has been run by the same family since 1926 — has been many things: a post office, a mechanic's workshop, a fuel stop, a lolly shop, a watering hole, and above all, a place where a community comes together.

image.altText: A black and white image of a woman reaching for something behind the counter of a regional general store.

items.title: Driver sentenced to 13 years' jail over hit-and-run of Canberra schoolboys

children.content: It's almost a year to the day since Tayler Hazell drove a stolen car erratically through Canberra, seriously injuring two children.

image.altText: Three police officers stand talking seriously next to a road with cones on it.

items.title: Apocalyptic scenes from Australia ahead of cyclone make worldwide news

children.content: Cyclone Narelle turned the sky over parts of Western Australia's north into an ominous red, sparking headlines across the globe.

gallery.altText: The streets of shark bay are cast in an eerie cloud of red due to TC Narelle

thumbnail.altText: The streets of shark bay are cast in an eerie cloud of red due to TC Narelle
A beachfront with a red atmosphere.
A small town with a red atmosphere.

items.title: Mining company found guilty over worker's 2021 death

children.content: A Queensland mining company has been found
guilty of industrial manslaughter over the death of a worker at a central Queensland mine site.

image.altText: Photos of 60 year old man with closely shaved hair, tanned  weathered skin

items.title: Secret tunnels used in plot to smuggle one tonne of cocaine into Australia, police say

children.content: Australian police have charged six crew members of the high-profile MV Raider vessel with drug smuggling, saying they attempted to bring 1 tonne of cocaine into Australia after being intercepted by French Polynesian authorities earlier this year.

image.altText: A police officer looking through a tunnel

items.title: Trump has 'no problem' with Russian oil shipment to Cuba

children.content: President Donald Trump has said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a US oil blockade.

image.altText: A man sits around a collection of jerry cans as another man lifts a clear container of water, moving to put it in a bag.

items.title: Townsville mother found not guilty of manslaughter over drowning of two sons

children.content: Townsville mother Leeanne Eatts has been found not guilty of the manslaughter of her two sons who drowned in the Ross River in February 2019.

image.altText: A woman in a blue shirt leads a group of people out of a courthouse.

items.title: Which states have introduced free public transport amid fuel crisis

children.content: As fuel prices continue to rise some states have moved to make public transport free, while others say that fares are already low enough. Find out where.

gallery.altText: Train crosses over bridge above water with view of Brisbane city in the background
A line up of people wait to get on a bus at a bus stop.
A crowded train carriage picture from behind. Every seat is full. A woman stands, back to the camera.

thumbnail.altText: Train crosses over bridge above water with view of Brisbane city in the background
A line up of people wait to get on a bus at a bus stop.
A crowded train carriage picture from behind. Every seat is full. A woman stands, back to the camera.

items.title: Coroner finds paramedic who made 'man flu' comments failed to meet standards

children.content: A coroner has recommended the South Australian Ambulance Service consider implementing an inclusive language program after an inquest heard an Indian national was told he was overreacting with the "man flu" in the days before he died of chronic pneumonia.

image.altText: Paramedic in green work clothes with grey hair stands next to ambulance

items.title: National campdraft competition cancelled over soaring cost of fuel

children.content: One of Australia's biggest campdrafting events is postponed due to the fuel crisis, with organisers hoping to reschedule the event within a few months.

image.altText: A man on a horse chases a cow through a paddock.

items.title: Five easy seafood dinners for busy weeknights

children.content: Looking for tasty, hassle-free seafood meals to add to your menu?  Here are some of our favourites, from prawn pasta and tuna mornay to baked salmon and tuna cakes.

gallery.altText: A salmon fillet on a white plate sitting on top of quinoa, brown rice and watermelon salsa. It has been cooked in an air frye
Crispy tuna cakes on a plate with a small bowl of pea puree and serving platter of tuna cakes with lemon wedges nearby
A patterned plate of spaghetti with prawns, lemon, chilli and garlic by Julia Busuttil Nishimura. An easy spring meal.

avatar.alt: Portrait of journalist Daniel Johnson wearing a grey and maroon plaid shirt and crossing his arms.

items.title: Storm forward hospitalised after suspected stroke in 'medical episode'

children.content: The Melbourne Storm say Tui Kamikamica is receiving the best possible care after being rushed to a hospital following a "medical episode" this morning.

image.altText: Tui Kamikamica carries the ball

items.title: How to discuss additional work-from-home days with your employer

children.content: With rising fuel prices adding additional financial stress to household living costs, what are your rights when it comes to working from home — and what are the best ways to approach your employer about flexible work arrangements?

image.altText: A woman sits in front of a computer monitor at home, with her cat sitting on a windowsill

items.title: Decisions made more than a decade ago are coming home to roost

children.content: The early 2010s were the years of peak globalisation, when it seemed all trade barriers were on a one-way trip to elimination. That was before Donald Trump arrived and ruptured the global order.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit jacket wearing glasses

items.title: Photos explore psychological drama of Australian rural life

children.content: Western Australian documentary photographer Brad Rimmer's beautiful, melancholy images of Wheatbelt life sprawl through Fremantle gallery.

image.altText: Darkened corridor with series of black and white images overlaid with deep colour

items.title: Coroner calls for stricter gun laws after father-son murder-suicide

children.content: An inquest into a father-son murder-suicide has led the NSW Coroner to recommend that doctors be legally required to alert police if they have concerns about patients who own guns.

image.altText: man and boy

items.title: Victorian Liberal candidate denies withdrawing from upper house ticket

children.content: The Liberal Party claims Dinesh Gourisetty has withdrawn his candidacy a day after winning preselection to replace controversial MP Moira Deeming.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit smiles for the camera in front of a colourful background.

items.title: Commuters say buses 'absolutely packed' as free transport starts

children.content: Some commuters in Hobart say the buses have been busier than usual, as free public transport rolls out across Tasmania.

image.altText: People walk off a Metro Tas bus in Hobart's CBD.

items.title: As universities cut courses, spending on consultants and contractors nears $2b

children.content: Consultancies have been accused of infiltrating universities and wasting scarce public funds on questionable advice about cutting courses and jobs.

gallery.altText: A student walks by several others sitting in a grassy area. Behind them is a modern glass building.

thumbnail.altText: A student walks by several others sitting in a grassy area. Behind them is a modern glass building.

items.title: Former AFL player Nick Stevens found guilty of dishonesty charges

children.content: A jury has found AFL player Nick Stevens guilty of deceptively charging families for swimming pools they never received.

image.altText: man walking outside with phone in hand

items.title: 'I feel betrayed': New scam figures show Australians lost more than $2 billion

children.content: Australians lost more than $2 billion to scammers in 2025, the latest data from the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has revealed.

image.altText: A man wearing a hoodie holds a laptop

items.title: The best and worst things about being on reality TV, according to Guy Sebastian

children.content: Singing his way into the Australian public's hearts way back in 2003, Guy Sebastian has since had a fascinating and varied career. We catch up with the star to find out about the little things that keep him going — from his two sons to his favourite TV show.

image.altText: Guy Sebastian sits behind a table smiling. Behind him are shop mannequins and sale signs

items.title: Aussies cruise to ODI series win over West Indies with dominant second game

children.content: Australia takes an unassailable 2-0 lead over the West Indies with a cruisy 91-run victory in the second ODI at St Kitts' Warner Park.

image.altText: Beth Mooney hits out

items.title: Rare durian variety sells for $200 at auction

children.content: A rare fruit has snared top dollar at auction at a Far North Queensland festival over the weekend.
